subtopic: Arctic Geopolitics: Greenland and Northern Sea Route
title: Arctic Geopolitics: Greenland and Northern Sea Route
focus: Focuses on the strategic importance of the NSR and Greenland's ice sheet in the context of climate and trade.
rationale: Fallback section derived from packet-level grouping.
sectionExcerpt:
[Page 033]
Greenland. Neighboring countries: Its nearest neighbor is Canada’s Ellesmere Island, which is located 16 miles to the north; Iceland is its nearest European neighbor. Geographical Features: Two-thirds of Greenland lies above the Arctic Circle. Arctic climate sustains the island’s massive ice sheet, which covers about 80 percent of the island. The Ice Sheet is the second largest in the world after the Antarctic Ice Sheet. Significance: Strategic location for Arctic geopolitics (US has a base), Future Trans-Arctic Shipping, large deposits of rare earth elements (REEs), oil and gas, fresh water source, fisheries etc. Map context: Greenland (Den.), Nuuk, Arctic Ocean, Canada, United States, Iceland, Norway, Sweden, Finland, Russia. Northern Sea Route (NSR). India has proposed to conduct a joint feasibility study for operationalising the Northern Sea Route (NSR) with Norwegian agencies. About NSR or Northeast Passage (NEP): Location: Maritime route along northern coast of Russia, connecting Atlantic Ocean to Pacific Ocean via Arctic waters. Geography: NSR begins at the border between Barents Sea (near Norway) and Kara Sea and ends in the Bering Strait (between Russia and Alaska). Shortest route: NSR has a distance of 13,000 kilometres between Europe and Asia compared to the Suez Canal route of 21,000 kilometres. It will reduce distance between Asia and Europe by 10-14 days compared to the Suez Canal route. Ports: Five main ports (Sabetta, Dudinka, Khatanga, Tiksi, and Pevek). Polar Silk Road Initiative by: China. Map context: Route connects St. Petersburg to Vladivostok via the Arctic; comparison with existing route via Strait of Malacca and Suez Canal. Qatar. Qatar (Capital: Doha). Iran launched missile attacks at the al-Udeid Air Base in Qatar, the largest American base in West Asia.
